name: Effect v4 Migration: Cause flattened from recursive tree to flat array
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Cause flattened from recursive tree to flat array
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: Cause<E> changed from a recursive tree with six variants (Empty | Fail<E> | Die | Interrupt | Sequential<E> | Parallel<E>) to a flat wrapper around an array of Failure values. Only three failure variants remain: Fail, Die, and Interrupt. Empty, Sequential, and Parallel variants were removed.

v3 Pattern:
interface Cause<E> with variants: Empty | Fail<E> | Die | Interrupt | Sequential<E> | Parallel<E>
Pattern matching via cause._tag on all six variants with recursive traversal of left/right for Sequential/Parallel.

v4 Pattern:
interface Cause<E> { readonly failures: ReadonlyArray<Failure<E>> }
type Failure<E> = Fail<E> | Die | Interrupt
Iterate over cause.failures array, switch on failure._tag for Fail/Die/Interrupt only.

Reason: The recursive tree structure was complex to traverse and reason about. A flat array is simpler and eliminates the need for recursive pattern matching.

Affected Modules: Cause

name: Effect v4 Migration: Cause type guards renamed to failure-level guards
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Cause type guards renamed to failure-level guards
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: v3 cause-level type guards (isFailType, isDieType, isInterruptType, isEmptyType) replaced by failure-level guards that operate on individual Failure values.

v3 Pattern:
Cause.isEmptyType(cause)
Cause.isFailType(cause)
Cause.isDieType(cause)
Cause.isInterruptType(cause)
Cause.isSequentialType(cause)
Cause.isParallelType(cause)

v4 Pattern:
cause.failures.length === 0 (replaces isEmptyType)
Cause.failureIsFail(failure)
Cause.failureIsDie(failure)
Cause.failureIsInterrupt(failure)
isSequentialType and isParallelType are removed entirely.

Reason: Guards now operate on individual Failure values from the flat array rather than on the Cause tree structure.

Affected Modules: Cause

name: Effect v4 Migration: Cause-level predicates renamed (isFailure, isDie, isInterrupted)
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Cause-level predicates renamed (isFailure, isDie, isInterrupted)
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: Cause-level predicates that check whether any failure of a given kind exists have been renamed.

v3 Pattern:
Cause.isFailure(cause)
Cause.isDie(cause)
Cause.isInterrupted(cause)
Cause.isInterruptedOnly(cause)

v4 Pattern:
Cause.hasFail(cause)
Cause.hasDie(cause)
Cause.hasInterrupt(cause)
Cause.isInterruptedOnly(cause) (unchanged)

Reason: Renamed for clarity - "has" prefix better communicates that the predicate checks for the existence of a failure type in the array.

Affected Modules: Cause

name: Effect v4 Migration: Cause.sequential and Cause.parallel replaced by Cause.merge
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Cause.sequential and Cause.parallel replaced by Cause.merge
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: Cause.sequential(left, right) and Cause.parallel(left, right) constructors both replaced by Cause.merge(left, right), which concatenates the failures arrays.

v3 Pattern:
Cause.sequential(left, right)
Cause.parallel(left, right)

v4 Pattern:
Cause.merge(left, right)

Reason: The distinction between sequential and parallel composition is no longer represented in the flattened data structure. Both just concatenate failure arrays.

Affected Modules: Cause

name: Effect v4 Migration: New Cause constructors (fromFailures, failureFail, failureDie, failureInterrupt)
episode_body:
Title: Migration: New Cause constructors (fromFailures, failureFail, failureDie, failureInterrupt)
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: New constructors added for working with the flattened Cause structure.

v3 Pattern:
No equivalent - constructed causes via Cause.fail, Cause.die, Cause.interrupt, Cause.sequential, Cause.parallel.

v4 Pattern:
Cause.fromFailures(failures) - construct a Cause from an array of Failure values.
Cause.failureFail(error) - construct a Fail Failure value.
Cause.failureDie(defect) - construct a Die Failure value.
Cause.failureInterrupt(fiberId) - construct an Interrupt Failure value.

Reason: These constructors support building causes from the new flat Failure array structure.

Affected Modules: Cause

name: Effect v4 Migration: Cause.annotate and Cause.filter* functions added
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Cause.annotate and Cause.filter* functions added
Category: migration
Source: cause.md

Change: New utility functions added to Cause module.

v3 Pattern:
No direct equivalent.

v4 Pattern:
Cause.annotate(annotations) - attach annotations to a Cause.
Cause.filterError(cause) - extract Fail failure types using the Filter module.
Cause.filterDefect(cause) - extract Die failure types using the Filter module.
Cause.filterInterrupt(cause) - extract Interrupt failure types using the Filter module.

Reason: Provides structured filtering and annotation capabilities for the new flat cause structure.

Affected Modules: Cause, Filter

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchAll renamed to Effect.catch. The general pattern is catchAll* shortened to catch*.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.fail("error").pipe(Effect.catchAll((error) => Effect.succeed(`recovered: ${error}`)))

v4 Pattern:
Effect.fail("error").pipe(Effect.catch((error) => Effect.succeed(`recovered: ${error}`)))

Reason: Shortened naming convention - catchAll* becomes catch*.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchAllCause renamed to Effect.catchCause
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchAllCause renamed to Effect.catchCause
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchAllCause renamed to Effect.catchCause.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.die("defect").pipe(Effect.catchAllCause((cause) => Effect.succeed("recovered")))

v4 Pattern:
Effect.die("defect").pipe(Effect.catchCause((cause) => Effect.succeed("recovered")))

Reason: Shortened naming convention - catchAll* becomes catch*.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchAllDefect renamed to Effect.catchDefect
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchAllDefect renamed to Effect.catchDefect
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchAllDefect renamed to Effect.catchDefect.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.catchAllDefect(handler)

v4 Pattern:
Effect.catchDefect(handler)

Reason: Shortened naming convention - catchAll* becomes catch*.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchSome renamed to Effect.catchFilter
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchSome renamed to Effect.catchFilter using Filter module
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchSome replaced by Effect.catchFilter. Instead of returning Option<Effect>, uses the Filter module.

v3 Pattern:
import { Effect, Option } from "effect"
Effect.fail(42).pipe(Effect.catchSome((error) => error === 42 ? Option.some(Effect.succeed("caught")) : Option.none()))

v4 Pattern:
import { Effect, Filter } from "effect"
Effect.fail(42).pipe(Effect.catchFilter(Filter.fromPredicate((error: number) => error === 42), (error) => Effect.succeed("caught")))

Reason: The catchSome* family is replaced by catchFilter / catchCauseFilter using the Filter module for more composable error filtering.

Affected Modules: Effect, Filter

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchSomeCause renamed to Effect.catchCauseFilter
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchSomeCause renamed to Effect.catchCauseFilter
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchSomeCause renamed to Effect.catchCauseFilter.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.catchSomeCause(handler)

v4 Pattern:
Effect.catchCauseFilter(filter, handler)

Reason: Consistent with the catchFilter pattern using the Filter module.

Affected Modules: Effect, Filter

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchSomeDefect removed
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchSomeDefect removed
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchSomeDefect has been removed in v4 with no direct replacement.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.catchSomeDefect(handler)

v4 Pattern:
Removed. Use Effect.catchDefect with conditional logic, or Effect.catchCauseFilter.

Reason: Simplified error handling API surface.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.catchTag and Effect.catchTags unchanged
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.catchTag and Effect.catchTags unchanged
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchTag and Effect.catchTags remain unchanged in v4. Effect.catchIf also unchanged.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.catchTag(tag, handler)
Effect.catchTags(cases)
Effect.catchIf(predicate, handler)

v4 Pattern:
Effect.catchTag(tag, handler) (unchanged)
Effect.catchTags(cases) (unchanged)
Effect.catchIf(predicate, handler) (unchanged)

Reason: These APIs were already well-designed and did not need renaming.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: New Effect.catchReason and Effect.catchReasons
episode_body:
Title: Migration: New Effect.catchReason and Effect.catchReasons for nested error causes
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: New error handling combinators for catching specific reasons within tagged errors.

v3 Pattern:
No equivalent.

v4 Pattern:
Effect.catchReason(errorTag, reasonTag, handler) - catches a specific reason within a tagged error without removing the parent error from the error channel. Useful for handling nested error causes (e.g. an AiError with a reason: RateLimitError | QuotaExceededError).
Effect.catchReasons(errorTag, cases) - like catchReason but handles multiple reason tags at once via an object of handlers.

Reason: Enables fine-grained error handling for errors with nested reason/cause fields.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: New Effect.catchEager optimization variant
episode_body:
Title: Migration: New Effect.catchEager optimization variant
Category: migration
Source: error-handling.md

Change: Effect.catchEager(handler) added as an optimization variant of Effect.catch.

v3 Pattern:
No equivalent.

v4 Pattern:
Effect.catchEager(handler) - evaluates synchronous recovery effects immediately rather than scheduling them.

Reason: Performance optimization for synchronous error recovery paths.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Fiber keep-alive built into core runtime
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Fiber keep-alive built into core runtime
Category: migration
Source: fiber-keep-alive.md

Change: The keep-alive mechanism that prevents Node.js process exit while fibers are suspended is now built into the core Effect runtime. Previously required runMain from @effect/platform-node.

v3 Pattern:
import { NodeRuntime } from "@effect/platform-node"
NodeRuntime.runMain(program) // needed to prevent process exit during fiber suspension

v4 Pattern:
Effect.runPromise(program) // process stays alive automatically while fibers are suspended
// runMain is still recommended for signal handling, exit codes, and error reporting

Reason: In v3, when a fiber suspended (e.g. on Deferred.await) and there was no other event loop work, Node.js would exit. The only workaround was runMain which installed a setInterval timer. v4 builds reference-counted keep-alive into the core runtime.

Affected Modules: Effect, Runtime

name: Effect v4 Migration: runMain still recommended for signal handling and exit codes
episode_body:
Title: Migration: runMain still recommended for signal handling and exit codes
Category: migration
Source: fiber-keep-alive.md

Change: Although keep-alive is now built-in, runMain from platform packages is still the recommended way to run Effect programs.

v3 Pattern:
import { NodeRuntime } from "@effect/platform-node"
NodeRuntime.runMain(program)

v4 Pattern:
runMain is still recommended because it provides: signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM with graceful fiber interruption), exit code management (process.exit(code) on failure), and error reporting (unhandled errors to console).

Reason: Keep-alive only solves process lifetime. runMain provides additional production-critical features.

Affected Modules: Runtime

name: Effect v4 Migration: FiberRef removed, replaced by ServiceMap.Reference
episode_body:
Title: Migration: FiberRef removed, replaced by ServiceMap.Reference
Category: migration
Source: fiberref.md

Change: FiberRef, FiberRefs, FiberRefsPatch, and Differ have been removed. Fiber-local state is now handled by ServiceMap.Reference, the same mechanism used for services with default values.

v3 Pattern:
import { FiberRef } from "effect"
// FiberRef was a dedicated type for fiber-local mutable state

v4 Pattern:
import { ServiceMap } from "effect"
// ServiceMap.Reference replaces FiberRef - same mechanism as services with defaults

Reason: Unified the fiber-local state mechanism with the service system. ServiceMap.Reference serves both purposes.

Affected Modules: FiberRef (removed), ServiceMap, References

name: Effect v4 Migration: Built-in FiberRef values moved to References module
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Built-in FiberRef values moved to References module
Category: migration
Source: fiberref.md

Change: All built-in FiberRef values are now ServiceMap.Reference values exported from the References module.

v3 Pattern:
FiberRef.currentConcurrency
FiberRef.currentLogLevel
FiberRef.currentMinimumLogLevel
FiberRef.currentLogAnnotations
FiberRef.currentLogSpan
FiberRef.currentScheduler
FiberRef.currentMaxOpsBeforeYield
FiberRef.currentTracerEnabled
FiberRef.unhandledErrorLogLevel

v4 Pattern:
References.CurrentConcurrency
References.CurrentLogLevel
References.MinimumLogLevel
References.CurrentLogAnnotations
References.CurrentLogSpans
References.Scheduler
References.MaxOpsBeforeYield
References.TracerEnabled
References.UnhandledLogLevel

Reason: FiberRef replaced by ServiceMap.Reference. Built-in references live in the References module.

Affected Modules: FiberRef (removed), References

name: Effect v4 Migration: FiberRef.get replaced by yielding Reference directly
Effect v4 Migration: Effect.locally replaced by Effect.provideService for references
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.locally replaced by Effect.provideService for references
Category: migration
Source: fiberref.md

Change: Scoped FiberRef updates via Effect.locally replaced by Effect.provideService with the reference.

v3 Pattern:
import { Effect, FiberRef, LogLevel } from "effect"
const program = Effect.locally(myEffect, FiberRef.currentLogLevel, LogLevel.Debug)

v4 Pattern:
import { Effect, References } from "effect"
const program = Effect.provideService(myEffect, References.CurrentLogLevel, "Debug")

Reason: References are services, so providing a value uses the same Effect.provideService mechanism as any other service.

Affected Modules: Effect, References

name: Effect v4 Migration: FiberRef.set replaced by Effect.provideService scoping
Effect v4 Migration: Effect.fork renamed to Effect.forkChild
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.fork renamed to Effect.forkChild
Category: migration
Source: forking.md

Change: Effect.fork renamed to Effect.forkChild for clarity about the parent-child relationship.

v3 Pattern:
import { Effect } from "effect"
const fiber = Effect.fork(myEffect)

v4 Pattern:
import { Effect } from "effect"
const fiber = Effect.forkChild(myEffect)

Reason: The name forkChild makes the parent-child fiber relationship explicit.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.forkDaemon renamed to Effect.forkDetach
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.forkDaemon renamed to Effect.forkDetach
Category: migration
Source: forking.md

Change: Effect.forkDaemon renamed to Effect.forkDetach for clarity.

v3 Pattern:
import { Effect } from "effect"
const fiber = Effect.forkDaemon(myEffect)

v4 Pattern:
import { Effect } from "effect"
const fiber = Effect.forkDetach(myEffect)

Reason: The name forkDetach better describes that the fiber is detached from the parent lifecycle.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.forkScoped and Effect.forkIn unchanged
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.forkScoped and Effect.forkIn unchanged
Category: migration
Source: forking.md

Change: Effect.forkScoped (fork tied to current Scope) and Effect.forkIn (fork in a specific Scope) remain unchanged in v4.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.forkScoped(myEffect)
Effect.forkIn(myEffect, scope)

v4 Pattern:
Effect.forkScoped(myEffect) (unchanged)
Effect.forkIn(myEffect, scope) (unchanged)

Reason: These APIs were already well-named and did not need changes.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect.forkAll and Effect.forkWithErrorHandler removed
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Effect.forkAll and Effect.forkWithErrorHandler removed
Category: migration
Source: forking.md

Change: Effect.forkAll and Effect.forkWithErrorHandler have been removed in v4.

v3 Pattern:
Effect.forkAll(effects) // fork all effects as child fibers
Effect.forkWithErrorHandler(effect, handler) // fork with error handler

v4 Pattern:
For forkAll: fork effects individually with Effect.forkChild or use higher-level concurrency combinators.
For forkWithErrorHandler: observe the fiber's result via Fiber.join or Fiber.await for error handling.

Reason: Simplified forking API surface. These utilities can be composed from the remaining primitives.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Fork options object added (startImmediately, uninterruptible)
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Fork options object added (startImmediately, uninterruptible)
Category: migration
Source: forking.md

Change: All fork variants (forkChild, forkDetach, forkScoped, forkIn) now accept an optional options object.

v3 Pattern:
No options available on fork combinators.

v4 Pattern:
Effect.forkChild(myEffect, { startImmediately: true })
Effect.forkChild(myEffect, { uninterruptible: true })
Effect.forkChild(myEffect, { uninterruptible: "inherit" })
// Also works curried:
myEffect.pipe(Effect.forkChild({ startImmediately: true }))

Options:
- startImmediately: boolean - when true, forked fiber begins executing immediately rather than being deferred
- uninterruptible: boolean | "inherit" - controls whether the forked fiber can be interrupted

Reason: Provides fine-grained control over fiber startup behavior without needing separate combinators.

Affected Modules: Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Layer memoization now shared across Effect.provide calls
Effect v4 Migration: Layer.fresh still available for bypassing memoization
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Layer.fresh still available for bypassing memoization
Category: migration
Source: layer-memoization.md

Change: Layer.fresh continues to work in v4 for cases where you want a layer built fresh, bypassing the shared cache.

v3 Pattern:
Layer.fresh(MyServiceLayer) // existed in v3

v4 Pattern:
Layer.fresh(MyServiceLayer) // still works - always builds with fresh memo map
Effect.provide(Layer.fresh(MyServiceLayer)) // bypasses shared cache

Reason: Some use cases (test isolation, independent resource pools) require fresh layer builds.

Affected Modules: Layer

name: Effect v4 Migration: New Effect.provide local option for isolated memoization
episode_body:
Title: Migration: New Effect.provide local option for isolated memoization
Category: migration
Source: layer-memoization.md

Change: Effect.provide now accepts a { local: true } option to build the provided layer with a local memo map instead of the fiber's shared one.

v3 Pattern:
No equivalent - each provide call already had its own memo map.

v4 Pattern:
Effect.provide(MyServiceLayer, { local: true })
// Builds layer and all sublayers from scratch, not shared with other provide calls

Reason: Provides explicit control for cases like test harnesses where each test should get independent resources, without needing Layer.fresh on every sublayer.

Affected Modules: Effect, Layer

name: Effect v4 Migration: Runtime<R> type removed, replaced by ServiceMap<R>
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Runtime<R> type removed, replaced by ServiceMap<R>
Category: migration
Source: runtime.md

Change: The Runtime<R> type that bundled Context<R>, RuntimeFlags, and FiberRefs has been removed. Use ServiceMap<R> instead. Run functions now live directly on Effect.

v3 Pattern:
interface Runtime<in R> {
  readonly context: Context.Context<R>
  readonly runtimeFlags: RuntimeFlags
  readonly fiberRefs: FiberRefs
}

v4 Pattern:
Runtime<R> no longer exists. Use ServiceMap<R> instead.
Run functions live directly on Effect.
The Runtime module now only contains: Teardown (interface), defaultTeardown, makeRunMain.

Reason: Runtime was a complex bundle of concerns. ServiceMap handles the service/context aspect, and RuntimeFlags/FiberRefs are handled differently in v4.

Affected Modules: Runtime (reduced), ServiceMap, Effect

name: Effect v4 Migration: Context.GenericTag replaced by ServiceMap.Service
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Context.GenericTag replaced by ServiceMap.Service
Category: migration
Source: services.md

Change: Context.GenericTag<T>(id) replaced by ServiceMap.Service<T>(id) for defining services with function syntax.

v3 Pattern:
import { Context } from "effect"
interface Database { readonly query: (sql: string) => string }
const Database = Context.GenericTag<Database>("Database")

v4 Pattern:
import { ServiceMap } from "effect"
interface Database { readonly query: (sql: string) => string }
const Database = ServiceMap.Service<Database>("Database")

Reason: Context module replaced by ServiceMap. Services are now defined via ServiceMap.Service.

Affected Modules: Context (removed), ServiceMap

name: Effect v4 Migration: Context.Tag class syntax replaced by ServiceMap.Service class syntax
Effect v4 Migration: Effect.Tag accessors removed, replaced by Service.use
Effect v4 Migration: Effect.Service replaced by ServiceMap.Service with make option
Effect v4 Migration: Context.Reference replaced by ServiceMap.Reference
Effect v4 Migration: Context module replaced by ServiceMap module
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Context module replaced by ServiceMap module (full API mapping)
Category: migration
Source: services.md

Change: The entire Context module is replaced by ServiceMap. All Context operations have ServiceMap equivalents.

v3 Pattern:
Context.GenericTag<T>(id) -> ServiceMap.Service<T>(id)
Context.Tag(id)<Self, Shape>() -> ServiceMap.Service<Self, Shape>()(id)
Context.Reference<Self>()(id, opts) -> ServiceMap.Reference<T>(id, opts)
Context.make(tag, impl) -> ServiceMap.make(tag, impl)
Context.get(ctx, tag) -> ServiceMap.get(map, tag)
Context.add(ctx, tag, impl) -> ServiceMap.add(map, tag, impl)
Context.mergeAll(...) -> ServiceMap.mergeAll(...)

v4 Pattern:
All operations above use ServiceMap instead of Context.

Reason: ServiceMap is a typed map from service identifiers to implementations, replacing the Context data structure.

Affected Modules: Context (removed), ServiceMap

name: Effect v4 Migration: Layer naming convention changed from Default/Live to layer
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Layer naming convention changed from Default/Live to layer
Category: migration
Source: services.md

Change: v4 adopts the convention of naming layers with lowercase 'layer' instead of v3's 'Default' or 'Live'.

v3 Pattern:
Logger.Default // or Logger.Live
Config.Default

v4 Pattern:
Logger.layer // primary layer
Logger.layerTest // test variant
Logger.layerConfig // config variant

Reason: Consistent naming convention. Use 'layer' for the primary layer and descriptive suffixes for variants.

Affected Modules: Layer, ServiceMap

name: Effect v4 Migration: Effect subtyping replaced by Yieldable trait
Effect v4 Migration: Types that implement Yieldable in v4
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Types that implement Yieldable in v4
Category: migration
Source: yieldable.md

Change: Specific types that implement the Yieldable interface (can be used with yield* but not as Effect directly).

v3 Pattern:
All these types extended Effect directly.

v4 Pattern:
Types implementing Yieldable:
- Effect itself
- Option - yields the value or fails with NoSuchElementError
- Result - yields the success or fails with the error
- Config - yields the config value or fails with ConfigError
- ServiceMap.Service - yields the service from the environment

Reason: These types have natural Effect conversions that make sense for yield* usage.

Affected Modules: Effect, Option, Result, Config, ServiceMap

name: Effect v4 Migration: Ref no longer extends Effect, use Ref.get
Effect v4 Migration: Deferred no longer extends Effect, use Deferred.await
Effect v4 Migration: Fiber no longer extends Effect, use Fiber.join
Effect v4 Migration: Yieldable types require .asEffect() for Effect combinators
Effect v4 Migration: Either replaced by Result in Yieldable types
episode_body:
Title: Migration: Either replaced by Result in Yieldable types
Category: migration
Source: yieldable.md

Change: The yieldable.md migration guide lists 'Result' (not 'Either') as a Yieldable type that yields the success or fails with the error. This indicates Either may have been renamed or replaced by Result in v4.

v3 Pattern:
Either<A, E> was an Effect subtype in v3.

v4 Pattern:
Result implements Yieldable - yields the success or fails with the error.

Reason: Part of the broader Yieldable refactor. Result is listed as the Yieldable type for success/error values.

Affected Modules: Either (possibly renamed), Result
